Rhinodiol rosea, or Rhodiola rosea, is a plant species in the Crassulaceae family, commonly known as the golden root or Arctic root. Rhodiola rosea has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, and it is believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving mental performance, and enhancing mood. The bioactive constituents of Rhodiola rosea include rosavin, rosin, and salidroside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rhodiola rosea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and teas, as well as in supplements and extracts.
